reasoning: The base rate for a militant group officially announcing its own disarmament is very low. Even though a ceasefire (Phase 1) was implemented in October 2025, returning all hostages, Hamas has repeatedly and consistently rejected demands to disarm as part of Phase 2. As recently as late October 2025, senior Hamas officials have stated they will not give up their weapons without the establishment of a fully sovereign Palestinian state, and that they intend to maintain security control in Gaza during a transition phase. Despite heavy international pressure and a Polymarket price of 21% on October 30, Hamas's core ideology strongly equates disarmament with "erasure" or surrender. Given the extremely tight timeline (under two months until Dec 31) and Hamas's consistent public denials of any willingness to demilitarize, the likelihood of an official, credible announcement of disarmament before year-end is very small.
